items.title: Bias–variance decomposition of error
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  err["Expected squared error E[(y - y_hat)^2]"]
  bias2["Bias^2: (E[y_hat] - f*(x))^2"]
  var["Variance: E[(y_hat - E[y_hat])^2]"]
  noise["Irreducible error: Var(epsilon)"]
  err --> bias2
  err --> var
  err --> noise
items.caption: Bias² captures systematic miss from model assumptions; variance captures training-set sensitivity; noise remains even with a perfect model.

items.title: High bias leads to underfitting
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  simple["Too-simple model class"]
  hb["High bias"]
  uf["Underfitting"]
  bad["High train AND val error"]
  simple --> hb --> uf --> bad
items.caption: Restrictive hypotheses produce systematically wrong average predictions — both training and validation performance suffer.

items.title: Reducing bias vs increasing variance
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  cap["Increase model capacity"]
  lb["Lower bias"]
  hv["Higher variance risk"]
  reg["Stronger regularization"]
  hb["Higher bias"]
  lv["Lower variance"]
  cap --> lb --> hv
  reg --> hb --> lv
items.caption: Flexibility reduces bias but can raise variance; regularization does the opposite — the core tradeoff with variance.
